skill.name: safe-debug
skill.description: Conservative debugging skill for deep learning research. It diagnoses tracebacks, CUDA OOM errors, checkpoint mismatches, shape mismatches, NaN loss, and training failures before any patching.
skill.summary: Diagnose deep learning research failures safely before changing code.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 22 potential issues, but the weak-crypto and shell-execution findings are false positives from prose, Markdown backticks, and Python formatted strings. No network access, subprocess execution, eval, environment access, credential handling, or prompt-injection text was found. The script does perform local filesystem reads and writes, so the final assessment is low risk rather than fully safe.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Read And Write
low_findings.description: The script reads a user-provided error file path and writes diagnostic outputs to a chosen output directory. This is consistent with the skill purpose and has no network or command execution path, but users should avoid pointing it at sensitive files or shared system directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads from --error-file and writes output files under --output-dir. The behavior is intentional and local-only, so the impact is limited.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Findings
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic imports, hashing calls, encryption calls, or credential handling. The flagged lines contain descriptive text or argument parser setup, not cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain no cryptographic API usage or weak algorithm selection. The static labels are inconsistent with the actual line contents.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Shell Execution Static Findings
low_findings.description: The shell execution detections are Markdown code spans and Python formatted strings used in generated diagnostic text. No subprocess, os.system, shell=True, eval, or command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are formatting delimiters inside text, not executable shell syntax. Imports and code paths show no external command runner.

low_findings.title: Parent Directory References In Documentation
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are relative documentation references that point to shared policy files outside this skill folder. They are not executed by the Python script, but marketplace packaging should ensure referenced files are available and reviewed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences are real, but they appear only in Markdown guidance. This creates packaging and review ambiguity rather than a confirmed path traversal exploit.

content.value_statement: Deep learning debugging can lead to speculative patches that change experiment meaning. This skill provides diagnosis-first guidance with approval gates before code changes.
actual_capabilities: Classifies pasted errors into CUDA OOM, checkpoint mismatch, distributed, device, shape, NaN, file missing, runtime, or unknown categories.
Suggests conservative next actions based on the detected failure category.
Creates a diagnosis document with the category, error excerpt, and analysis summary.
Creates a patch plan that keeps code changes blocked until explicit approval.
Creates a status file that records patch authorization and savepoint recommendations.
limitations: Classification is keyword-based and may miss unusual or multi-cause failures.
It does not run training jobs, inspect tensors, or reproduce failures automatically.
It reads local error text and writes local reports, so file paths should be chosen carefully.
It is focused on deep learning research, not general application debugging.

use_cases.title: Triage Training Failures
use_cases.description: Classify a traceback and get conservative next checks before changing model or training code.

use_cases.title: Review Checkpoint Problems
use_cases.description: Analyze missing key, unexpected key, or tensor size mismatch errors before adding compatibility code.

use_cases.title: Investigate Unstable Runs
use_cases.description: Use structured guidance for NaN loss, device mismatch, or distributed training symptoms.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose A Basic Error
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug to diagnose this error and suggest the smallest safe next step: RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a pasted traceback

prompt_templates.title: Check A Model Load Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug on this checkpoint loading failure. Explain the likely cause and do not suggest code edits without approval.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate NaN Loss
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply safe-debug to a training run where loss becomes NaN after three epochs. Include likely causes and safe validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Full Failure Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run safe-debug on this full traceback and command history. Produce a diagnosis, patch plan, and savepoint recommendation before any mutation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit before changing research code

output_examples.input: A CUDA out-of-memory traceback during model training.
output: Suggested next checks include batch size, input resolution, and mixed precision settings.
A configuration-only reduction is preferred before architecture changes.

output_examples.input: A checkpoint load error with missing keys and unexpected keys.
output: Savepoint is recommended before any patch.
Suggested next checks include checkpoint source, model variant, and load strictness assumptions.
Compatibility code should wait until the mismatch is confirmed as expected.

output_examples.input: A training run where loss becomes NaN after several epochs.
output: Savepoint is recommended before code changes.
Suggested next checks include data ranges, loss inputs, mixed precision, and learning rate.
A short controlled run can help identify when the instability starts.

best_practices: Provide the full traceback, launch command, and recent configuration changes when possible.
Review the diagnosis and patch plan before approving any repository change.
Create a branch or savepoint before medium-risk or high-risk fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for broad refactors without a concrete failure.
Do not treat a debug fix as a research contribution without checking experiment comparability.
Do not point input or output paths at sensitive files or shared system directories.

faq.question: What does this skill diagnose?
faq.answer: It diagnoses common deep learning research failures, including CUDA OOM, checkpoint mismatch, shape mismatch, NaN loss, and runtime errors.

faq.question: Does it modify code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to diagnose first and keep patching blocked until explicit approval.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found for external command execution. The reviewed script uses standard Python parsing, classification, and file writing.

faq.question: What files does it create?
faq.answer: It can create a diagnosis report, a patch plan, and a status report in the selected output directory.

faq.question: Is it only for PyTorch?
faq.answer: No. Some examples mention PyTorch-style failures, but the keyword categories can help with general deep learning research errors.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
